heading: Holidays; daily, hourly, and piece-work basis employees
label: 6104 Holidays; daily, hourly, and piece-work basis employees
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 86a, .June 29, 1938, ch. 818, § 152 Stat. 1246, .June 11, 1954, ch. 28368 Stat. 249, , .July 18, 1958Pub. L. 85–533, § 272 Stat. 377The enumeration of holidays is eliminated as unnecessary in view of section 6103.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1979— substituted “Mayor” for “Commissioner” in pars. (1) and (3), and “Council of the District of Columbia” for “District of Columbia Council” in par. (2).Pub. L. 96–541968— substituted “Commissioner” for “Board of Commissioners” in pars. (1) and (3), and “District of Columbia Council” for “Board of Commissioners” in par. (2).Pub. L. 90–623
Effective Date of 1979 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–54section 2(b) of Pub. L. 96–54section 305 of this titleJuly 12, 1979

Ex. Ord. No. 10552. Delegation of Authority To Promulgate RegulationsEx. Ord. No. 10552, , 19 F.R. 5079, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12107, , 44 F.R. 1055, provided:Aug. 10, 1954Dec. 28, 1978By virtue of the authority vested in me by of the United States Code, , it is declared that the Office of Personnel Management be, and it is hereby, designated and empowered to exercise, without the approval, ratification, or other action of the President, the authority vested in the President by the joint resolution of , , as amended by the act of , [this section], to promulgate regulations under which certain employees of the Government may be prevented or relieved from working by administrative order.section 301 of title 365 Stat. 71352 Stat. 124668 Stat. 249June 29, 1938June 11, 1954

children.text: on which agencies are closed by Executive order, or, for individuals employed by the government of the District of Columbia, by order of the Mayor;
by administrative order under regulations issued by the President, or, for individuals employed by the government of the District of Columbia, by the Council of the District of Columbia; or

solely because of the occurrence of a legal public holiday under , or a day declared a holiday by Federal statute, Executive order, or, for individuals employed by the government of the District of Columbia, by order of the Mayor;section 6103 of this title
